verified_points: Over 40 sustainable finance products; frameworks for transition finance, green and sustainable products, and sustainability bonds; USD157bn mobilised from Jan 2021 to Sept 2025 against a USD300bn target.

Bank states it offers sustainability-linked financing solutions, ESG-linked derivatives, sustainable deposits, financial institutional trade loans, and complex project financing including grid upgrades and renewable infrastructure.

publication_title: India’s energy transition: what corporates say
verified_points: Survey of 40 Indian corporates; 86 percent expect to use sustainable finance solutions in future; green and sustainability-linked bonds and loans identified as the most relevant instruments; interest in carbon credits expected to rise.

Standard Chartered positions sustainable trade finance as a sustainability-linked financing solution across trade and working-capital products; includes sustainable trade loans for financial institutions.

publication_title: Apraava Energy secures USD 92 million from British International Investment
verified_points: Verified India transaction combining Standard Chartered and BII funding for advanced metering infrastructure linked to India’s energy transition.
USD157bn sustainable finance mobilised from January 2021 to September 2025; USD300bn aspiration by 2030; sustainable finance includes bonds, loans, advisory and trade finance products.
Deutsche Bank shows a cumulative EUR900bn sustainable and transition finance target through year-end 2030 and EUR471bn achieved since January 2020 by Q4 2025.

publication_title: Deutsche Bank sets new 2030 sustainable and transition finance target and publishes its initial Transition Finance Framework
verified_points: Transition Finance Framework is positioned for hard-to-abate sectors; the updated 2030 cumulative target is EUR900bn.
India transition context from Deutsche Bank; highlights rising electricity demand, non-fossil capacity targets, and the scale of investment challenge.
Defines project finance as cash-flow-based non-recourse or limited-recourse funding via an SPV; explains why it suits capital-intensive infrastructure and renewable assets.

publication_title: REC: financing India’s renewable energy strategy (2)
verified_points: Describes India renewable financing mix spanning public sector banks, private banks, NBFIs, DFIs, green bonds, YieldCos, project finance, AR facilities, LCs and ECB loans.

publication_title: REC: supporting India’s renewable energy strategy (1)
verified_points: Highlights grid stability and storage needs as renewable penetration rises and notes capital intensity of renewable project development.
UBS says it partners with clients to mobilize capital toward a more sustainable world and frames sustainability through Protect, Grow and Attract strategic pillars.
UBS describes sustainability capabilities across institutional investors, corporates and private clients; Investment Bank focuses on advice and capital-markets support for the low-carbon transition.

Investment Bank facilitated 96 GSSS bond transactions in 2024 and states it laid groundwork for green structured issuance, blended finance, emission allowances and project-based carbon removals financing.
UBS Sustainable Finance Guideline covers green, social, sustainability, transition and sustainability-linked bonds and loans, plus sustainability-labeled equity instruments.

Defines sustainable loans as sustainability-linked loans and use-of-proceeds loans for corporate and institutional clients; basis for sustainable financing and sustainable investing volume disclosures.

publication_title: Green Funding Framework – Annual Investor Report 2024
verified_points: UBS Green Funding Framework supports funding products including bonds, derivatives and deposits and currently allocates proceeds to eligible green assets such as green buildings.
